channel.title: The Grant Desk
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk.

item.title: Realities in Transition: RiT 2 Open Call #2 - Telepresence in the Digital Space
item.description:
Organisation: Realities in Transition, in collaboration with MEET Digital Culture Center Milan

Location: Hybrid: online phases plus a two-month in-person period at MEET Digital Culture Center, Milan, Italy

Award: Three grants of EUR 15,000 each (one per artist), covering the artist's fee plus travel, accommodation and subsistence, disbursed in three installments (EUR 7,000 on signing, EUR 4,000 after the Test Lab, EUR 4,000 at the end). Production costs of up to EUR 5,000 are covered by the host, plus a mentoring programme. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month hybrid residency (online October-December 2026; in-person at MEET Milan January-February 2027; online February-March 2027), final project presented June 2027

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Open call from the Realities in Transition (RiT 2 - Unwritten Worlds) programme for artists and designers to co-create a collective XR artwork during a residency exploring telepresence and the shifting nature of presence in the digital age. The residency investigates how the body operates across physical and digital dimensions through motion capture and immersive environments, treating movement as a language that can be encoded, transformed and transmitted, and opening critical and speculative perspectives on authorship, identity and corporeality. MEDIUM: motion capture and immersivity. ELIGIBILITY: individual XR and immersive artists who are European residents; three artists are selected and joined by a creative technologist to work as a group. The residency alternates shared work, critical discussion and experimentation, from conception and prototyping to a public Test Lab and audience presentation, with a mentoring programme covering XR mediation, ethics and inclusion, accessibility, hybridisation of spaces, distribution and business models. Apply via the Realities in Transition open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America Literary Grants 2027
item.description:
Organisation: PEN America

Location: United States (US-based writers)

Award: Varies by grant (e.g. PEN/Jean Stein Grant for Literary Oral History, PEN/Phyllis Naylor Working Writer Fellowship), generally in the $5,000 to $10,000+ range. No application fee.

Duration: Project / works-in-progress support

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's suite of literary grants and fellowships supporting individual writers and works in progress across genres (fiction, nonfiction, poetry, oral history and more), each with its own focus and criteria. ELIGIBILITY: varies by grant; generally US-based writers, with several open to writers and translators at different career stages. Distinct from the PEN/Heim Translation Fund and PEN Presents. Review individual grant criteria and apply via the PEN America literary grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FUTO Microgrants
item.description:
Organisation: FUTO

Location: Remote (geographic eligibility not specified; verify before applying)

Award: One-time microgrants of $1,000 to $5,000 for small or early-stage projects. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

FUTO microgrants support small and early-stage projects challenging the dominance of large technology platforms, including privacy tools, open-source software and decentralisation initiatives. ELIGIBILITY: individuals and small early-stage projects; geographic eligibility is not specified, so confirm before applying. Applications are open on a rolling basis; apply via the FUTO grants page or by emailing grantapps@futo.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Fellowship (Digital and other spheres)
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Stuttgart, Germany

Award: Monthly stipend of EUR 1,300 for a 6 to 9 month residency, plus a furnished live/work studio, travel reimbursement, and (for non-EU residents) health insurance.

Duration: 6 to 9 month residency

Deadline: Rolling / undated

International, cross-disciplinary fellowship from Akademie Schloss Solitude in Stuttgart, offering a funded live/work residency across spheres including Art, Architecture/Design, Music/Sound, Performing Arts, Literature, Humanities/Social Sciences, and Digital. The Digital sphere explicitly covers algorithmic and networked technologies, open-source culture and post-digital aesthetics, making it a strong fit for AI / synthetic-media / critical-technology work (including writing projects). ELIGIBILITY: open to applicants of any nationality who are within 2 to 10 years of finishing their training/studies (or equivalent independent practice); no published-book or journalist requirement. CADENCE: recurring multi-year calls (the 2026-2028 call opened in October 2025); the next cycle is expected in roughly 2-3 years, so monitor the site. Selection is jury-based; an application fee applies in some cycles.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Bogliasco Foundation: Fellowship (Liguria Study Center)
item.description:
Organisation: Bogliasco Foundation

Location: Bogliasco, near Genoa, Italy

Award: Fully funded one-month residency: private accommodation, full board, and a private study/studio. NOTE: $30 application fee.

Duration: One-month residency

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fully funded one-month residency at the Bogliasco Foundation's Liguria Study Center near Genoa, supporting individuals doing advanced creative or scholarly work in the arts and humanities (including creative nonfiction and literature). ELIGIBILITY: open to individuals of all ages and nationalities; applicants should demonstrate significant achievement commensurate with their age and experience, so debut/emerging writers can apply if the work is strong; no published-book, contract or journalist requirement. APPLICATION: project proposal plus a work sample (about 25 pages of prose), CV and two references; there is a $30 application fee. CADENCE: two cycles per year (Fall and Spring rounds) - check the site for the current deadline.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rockefeller Foundation: Bellagio Center Residency
item.description:
Organisation: The Rockefeller Foundation (Bellagio Center)

Location: Bellagio, Lake Como, Italy

Award: Fully funded residency of roughly 26 days: private room/board and dedicated workspace, plus travel support.

Duration: Approximately 4-week residency

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fully funded residency at the Rockefeller Foundation's Bellagio Center on Lake Como for academics, artists and practitioners working in any discipline on projects with clear social impact. Recent priorities have included AI development that prioritizes human connection, equity and social good, making it a strong thematic fit for a critical project on synthetic media and society. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to academics, artists and practitioners across disciplines; no published-book or journalist requirement, though projects are expected to demonstrate social impact and the call is competitive. CADENCE: recurring annual open call - monitor the site for the current round.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arts Council England: Developing Your Creative Practice (DYCP) Round 24
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council England

Location: England, UK

Award: GBP 1,000 to GBP 12,000 per individual for creative-practice development (research, training, time to think, travel, mentoring, etc.). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (practice-development period)

Deadline: 02 Jul 2026

Developing Your Creative Practice (DYCP) supports individual creative and cultural practitioners in England to take time to develop their practice, across all disciplines including digital and media arts. The funding is for practice development (research, training, mentoring, travel, experimentation) rather than producing or presenting finished work. ELIGIBILITY: England-based independent creative practitioners with a track record who work outside the major-funded organisations. Round 24 opens 4 June 2026 (12:00) and closes 2 July 2026. Apply via the Arts Council England DYCP page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prototype Fund Class 03 (Open-Source Software)
item.description:
Organisation: Prototype Fund (Open Knowledge Foundation Germany)

Location: Germany / EU (lead must be based in Germany; funded members must reside in the EU)

Award: Up to EUR 95,000 over 6 months, or up to EUR 158,000 over 10 months, plus coaching. No application fee.

Duration: 6 or 10 months

Deadline: 30 Nov 2026

Prototype Fund supports the development of socially relevant open-source software, offering low-threshold funding and coaching to individual developers and small teams. ELIGIBILITY: individual developers or teams of up to four; funded members must reside in the EU and the lead entity must be based in Germany. Application window 1 October to 30 November 2026. Apply via the Prototype Fund application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Long-Term Future Fund (EA Funds)
item.description:
Organisation: Effective Altruism Funds

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: Individual stipends and project grants, typically ~$2,500 to $120,000+ (recent average around $38,000 per grant). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (varies)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Effective Altruism Funds' Long-Term Future Fund makes grants to individuals and small groups working to reduce risks from advanced AI and other threats to humanity's long-term future, including technical AI safety and alignment research, field-building and communication. ELIGIBILITY: individuals and small groups; not geographically restricted. Applications accepted on a rolling basis via the EA Funds application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prairie Ronde Artist Residency: Summer/Fall 2026 (Michigan)
item.description:
Organisation: The Mill at Vicksburg

Location: Vicksburg, Michigan, USA (5-6 week on-site residency, private housing)

Award: $2,000 stipend plus a $500 travel grant and private housing for the 5-6 week residency. Application fee: $25.

Duration: Five- to six-week residency

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026

Prairie Ronde Artist Residency at The Mill at Vicksburg, a self-directed residency open to artists across disciplines including film, video and new media. ELIGIBILITY: artists of any discipline; open worldwide. Apply via the Prairie Ronde application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Interledger Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Interledger Foundation

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: USD 72,000 personal stipend plus a USD 20,000 project budget. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship term (project-based)

Deadline: 02 Jul 2026

Interledger Foundation fellowship funding individuals to advance open, interoperable digital payment infrastructure (the Interledger Protocol and Open Payments) and broaden digital financial access. ELIGIBILITY: individual researchers, developers, open-source contributors, advocates, creators and artists working on digital financial inclusion; open worldwide. Apply via the Interledger fellowship page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship in Investigative Journalism 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Durham University, in partnership with Reuters

Location: Based in a Reuters newsroom (London, New York, Sydney or Toronto)

Award: Approximately GBP 4,444 per month salary (around GBP 53,333/year pro-rata) plus a GBP 1,250 monthly living stipend and GBP 1,800 travel budget. No application fee.

Duration: Nine-month fellowship

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Funded investigative-journalism fellowship from Durham University and Reuters, embedding the fellow in a major Reuters newsroom to pursue an in-depth investigation. ELIGIBILITY: journalists with roughly 2 to 5 years of professional experience; professionals from related investigative fields (authors, researchers, documentary or photo/video investigators) considered case by case; open to applicants worldwide. AI-generated proposals will be disqualified. Apply via the Durham University Sir Harry Evans Memorial Fund page by 10 July 2026, 12:00 BST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lux Capital Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Lux Capital

Location: United States (in-person retreats; remote project work)

Award: $15,000 non-dilutive grant (no strings attached, no need to incorporate a company), plus mentorship and all-expenses-paid retreats. No application fee.

Duration: Summer fellowship (kick-off late July; closes late August/early September 2026)

Deadline: 13 Jun 2026

Fellowship from venture-capital firm Lux Capital honouring exceptional undergraduates at the intersection of science and technology, pushing the boundaries of the physical, computational and life sciences. No prior experience required; flexible by design to run alongside other academic and professional commitments. FOCUS AREAS: Physical Sciences (next-gen materials, defense, chips, energy, space); Life Sciences (healthcare, computational biology, new therapeutics); and Computational Sciences (AI, cryptography, open source, infrastructure, dev tools). WHO THEY SEEK: scrappy, high-velocity builders who ship fast; deeply technical engineering and scientific pioneers (startup designation does not matter); and seekers with something to prove. BENEFITS: a $15,000 non-dilutive grant to offset project costs (no strings attached, no need to formally incorporate); a personalized mentor from the Lux portfolio and investment team; all-expenses-paid retreats at the start and end of the summer; and sessions ranging from fireside chats with portfolio founders to themed discussions. ELIGIBILITY: undergraduates (team applications accepted). TIMELINE: applications due June 13, 2026; results late June; fellowship kick-off late July; close late August/early September. Contact: flux@luxcapital.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Alan Andres Writing Fellowships 2026-2027 (Boston Public Library)
item.description:
Organisation: Associates of the Boston Public Library

Location: Boston / New England, United States

Award: Two fellowships (apply to only one): Writer-in-Residence: $70,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support and a year-long office residency at the Boston Public Library. Picture Book Writer Fellowship: $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support. No application fee.

Duration: Writer-in-Residence: one year (October 2026 to September 2027)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

The Associates of the Boston Public Library offer the Alan Andres Writing Fellowships for emerging New England writers, funded by an anonymous donor committed to writers earning a living wage. Applicants may apply to only one fellowship; winners are chosen through a blind judging process. (1) ALAN ANDRES WRITER-IN-RESIDENCE (22nd year): a year-long residency (October 2026 to September 2027) with an office at the Boston Public Library, a $70,000 stipend, and $2,500 for editorial support, to complete a manuscript for children or young adults (fiction, nonfiction, plays, poetry, or graphic novels); apply at www.writer-in-residence.org. (2) ALAN ANDRES PICTURE BOOK WRITER FELLOWSHIP (formerly the Illustrated Children's Book Fellowship, 2nd year): a $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 for editorial support to complete a manuscript for young readers (fiction, nonfiction, memoir, or poetry for ages 0-2 board books or ages 3-8 picture books and early readers); apply at www.associatesbpl.org/picturebook. ELIGIBILITY: emerging New England writers. Applications close June 5, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jan Michalski Foundation: Writers Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Jan Michalski Foundation for Writing and Literature

Location: Montricher, Switzerland (Swiss Jura, near Lausanne and Geneva)

Award: Weekly allowance of CHF 400, plus travel costs to and from home covered, accommodation in a private cabin, and breakfast and lunch provided. Electric bikes and library access included.

Duration: Two weeks to three months (first 2027 residents arrive mid-January 2027)

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Residency for writers and translators at the Jan Michalski Foundation in Montricher, Switzerland, set in seven distinctive 'cabin' modules hanging from an openwork canopy, offering conditions to start, continue or finish a writing project. Around forty authors from around the world (emerging to established) are hosted each year. Open to all kinds of writing and all languages, with priority given to literary writers and translators; other disciplines are welcome as long as literature is at the heart of the project. Residencies are for individuals or pairs working on a common project (e.g. a writer and a translator). A percentage of residencies are dedicated to nature writing. WHAT IS PROVIDED: a private cabin for independent living (a separate cabin per person for pairs; one accessible cabin for reduced mobility), travel costs to and from home, a weekly allowance of CHF 400, breakfast and lunch, electric bikes, and daytime library access. ELIGIBILITY: no age or nationality restrictions; beginners accepted; one application per year; former residents may not reapply (families, children and pets cannot be accommodated). Stays cannot be split into multiple periods. Selection is by a panel chaired by Vera Michalski-Hoffmann, assessing the literary quality of the project, the candidate's background, and whether the stay length matches the project scope. APPLICATION: online in English or French (work excerpts may be in any language); the 2027 form is open 2 June to 31 August 2026; selected candidates announced December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: LPM 2026 Rome: Call to Participate (Live Performers Meeting)
item.description:
Organisation: LPM - Live Performers Meeting

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: Paid open call, not a grant: participation requires an artist/guest subscription of EUR 20 (includes a pass for all activities, a T-shirt, a daily drink, and a 30-minute performance slot), plus 20% off workshops. The Live Immersive Contest offers prizes from technical partners.

Duration: Festival edition in Rome 2026

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Open call to participate in LPM (Live Performers Meeting), described as the biggest worldwide event dedicated to audiovisual performing arts, returning to Rome for its 27th edition. Artists can take part with AV performances, VJ sets and live mapping (a 30-minute slot to perform); workshops, lectures and project showcases; interactive AV installations; and the Live Immersive Contest (competing for prizes from technical partners). NOTE: this is a participation call rather than a funding opportunity - it requires a paid subscription (EUR 20 as artist or guest) which includes a festival pass, a 30-minute slot, a T-shirt and a daily drink, plus a 20% discount on workshops; there is also a volunteer option. Deadline to submit a proposal is 16 August 2026. Enquiries: subscriptions@liveperformersmeeting.net.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Library: Creative Fellowship 2026 - Remote Ecologies (Art, Climate and the Amazon)
item.description:
Organisation: The British Library

Location: London / UK-based

Award: GBP 7,500 stipend plus up to GBP 3,000 production budget.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

A collection-based artistic research fellowship at the British Library responding to climate and the Amazon through the Library's holdings. The fellow develops new work informed by the collections over roughly a year (September 2026 to July 2027), receiving a GBP 7,500 stipend and up to GBP 3,000 for production. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based artists, over 18, not currently a student, with a public track record and an interest in collection-based research and climate issues.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Hugo Burge Foundation: Grants for Creative Individuals 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Hugo Burge Foundation

Location: United Kingdom

Award: Up to GBP 5,000 per individual (can fund up to 100% of a project under GBP 10,000). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Project grants from the Hugo Burge Foundation supporting individual creatives across all disciplines based in the UK, with a focus on craftsmanship, creative communities and education. The Creative Individuals stream awards up to GBP 5,000 and can cover up to 100% of a project budget under GBP 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: practitioners in all creative disciplines based across the UK. Applications open 1 June 2026 via Submittable and close 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Trust: Grants for Artist Projects (GAP) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Artist Trust

Location: Washington State, United States

Award: $2,500 unrestricted project grant per artist (awarded to 65 artists). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

Unrestricted project-based grants of $2,500 from Artist Trust to 65 Washington State artists across all disciplines, including literary and media arts. Funds can support a specific project or career-advancing activity. ELIGIBILITY: Washington State residents who are originators of works of art and not current students; open to artist teams.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Beautiful Bizarre Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Beautiful Bizarre Magazine

Location: Worldwide (exhibition in San Francisco, USA)

Award: Grand Prize $10,000 plus category awards (e.g. Imaginative Realism $5,000) and a dedicated Digital Art Award and Emerging Artist Award; $76,000+ total in cash and prizes. NOTE: $40 entry fee per submission.

Duration: Annual prize (exhibition follows)

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

International, non-acquisitive art prize across seven categories including a dedicated Digital Art Award, with cash prizes and a San Francisco gallery exhibition. Grand Prize is $10,000, with category awards and over $76,000 in total cash and prizes. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally with no geographic restriction; all static media; includes an Emerging Artist Award. NOTE: there is a $40 entry fee per submission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Toronto Arts Council: Media Artists Program - Creation 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Toronto Arts Council

Location: Toronto, Canada

Award: Up to $15,000 (CAD) per artist for media-arts creation. No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026

Project funding from the Toronto Arts Council for professional media artists to create or complete independent film, video, audio, digital, VR/AR and multimedia works, with grants up to $15,000. ELIGIBILITY: Canadian citizens, permanent residents or Protected Persons who have lived in Toronto for at least one year, are professional media artists retaining full creative control, and are not students.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SFFILM Documentary Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SFFILM

Location: Remote / worldwide (need not be US-based)

Award: $10,000 to $20,000 per project (3 to 6 projects per year). Application fee: $30 (regular deadline) or $50 (final deadline).

Duration: Project-based grant

Deadline: 07 Jul 2026

SFFILM's Documentary Film Fund supports feature-length documentaries in post-production with compelling stories and an innovative approach to the craft. ELIGIBILITY: applicants 18 or older in a key creative role (director or producer); feature-length documentary (60+ minutes) within roughly three months of completing post-production; need not be US-based. Regular deadline 9 June 2026 ($30 fee); final deadline 7 July 2026 ($50 fee). Distinct from the SFFILM Rainin Grant. Apply via the SFFILM grants portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Agog: Climate Futures Open Call 2026 (Immersive Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Agog: Impact in Immersive Media

Location: Remote / project-based (US-registered entity or fiscal sponsor required; international collaborators allowed)

Award: $25,000 to $200,000 per grant (up to $1M total pool). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Open call from Agog for immersive-media projects that drive climate engagement and action, using augmented and mixed reality, spatial sound, smart glasses and related technologies. ELIGIBILITY: creators, artists and teams working in immersive media; applicants must apply through a US-registered entity or fiscal sponsor, though international collaborators are allowed. Apply via the Agog open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kate Medina Fellowship for Literary Narrative Nonfiction 2026 (NYPL)
item.description:
Organisation: New York Public Library, in partnership with Random House

Location: New York City, USA (on-site research at the NYPL Schwarzman Building)

Award: $30,000 stipend for four continuous months of research (September 2026 to March 2027). No application fee.

Duration: Four continuous months of research

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Fellowship from the New York Public Library and Random House for writers of literary narrative nonfiction whose projects engage NYPL's archival and special collections. ELIGIBILITY: writers of narrative nonfiction with a project requiring on-site access to the collections at the Stephen A. Schwarzman Building in New York. Apply via the NYPL fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pulitzer Center: U.S. Civil Society Microgrants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Pulitzer Center

Location: United States

Award: US$2,000 to US$4,000 (draft budget required)

Duration: Up to six months (late June to early December 2026); project closure and impact reporting by December 21, 2026

Deadline: 08 Jun 2026

Microgrant supporting U.S. civil society organizations and groups to build civic engagement projects that use Pulitzer Center-supported reporting to amplify the voices of affected communities, foster public debate, and drive meaningful audience engagement. Projects must engage at least one Pulitzer Center story and/or journalist and focus on one of three issue areas: Information and Artificial Intelligence (impact of AI on society, AI governance dialogue); Climate and the Environment (U.S. fisheries and fishing communities, or climate and labor); or Global Health (mental well-being, or connections to Global South reporting). Supported activities include multi-stakeholder dialogues, public forums, community engagement, creative campaigns (videos, infographics), and other innovative formats such as art exhibitions. Out of scope: projects not using Pulitzer Center reporting, direct advocacy or lobbying, partisan campaigns, and for-profit initiatives. ELIGIBILITY: grassroots organizations, academics and researchers, education institutions, civil society organizations and coalitions, youth movements, and other groups with past experience implementing activities in the United States, a track record of collaboration, and proven experience on the identified topics. APPLICATION: project description (max 400 words), target audience and projected reach, methodology, implementation timeline, preliminary budget, content production and distribution plan if applicable, lead applicant CV plus a letter of recommendation. Selection involves shortlisting and an interview with the lead applicant. A required onboarding meeting for grantees is Wednesday, June 24, 2026 (7:00-8:30pm EDT).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lanesboro Arts: Early Career Artist Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Lanesboro Arts

Location: Lanesboro, Minnesota, United States (pop. 754)

Award: $1,250 weekly stipend (fixed per residency, including for small collaborating teams); housing provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two or four week residency, scheduled within February 1 to December 31, 2027

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Residency program in the rural community of Lanesboro, MN, supported by the Jerome Foundation, offering early career artists two or four week residencies to explore how their work can engage the community and how a rural community can inform their work. Staff co-create a fully customized residency experience around community engagement. Lanesboro Arts offers two to three residencies per year; the program provides an individual studio plus access to conference rooms, desk space, a gallery, piano and stage, with private housing (apartment, kitchen, private room and bathroom). No meals provided. Open to all disciplines. ELIGIBILITY: legal residents of Minnesota or one of the five boroughs of New York City for at least one year prior to applying; defined as early career artists with at least a 2-year and no more than 10-year track record of creating and presenting their own projects (not first-time creators or beginning artists). Degree-seeking students at the time of application or during the grant period (K-12, college, graduate or postgraduate) are not eligible. Age is not a factor. The primary goal must be to generate new work rather than remount or re-interpret existing work. Apply via the online webform on the Lanesboro Arts website. Selection announced June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ICCI ART VALLEY: International Visiting Art Scholars 2026
item.description:
Organisation: USC-SJTU Institute of Cultural and Creative Industry (ICCI), Shanghai Jiao Tong University

Location: Shanghai, China

Award: Minimum RMB 8,000 one-time project grant (for production and lecture/workshop materials), plus round-trip economy airfare from the artist's departure city, accommodation, and group-exhibition installation costs. Note: a one-time RMB 8,000 program fee applies. Requires donating two artworks to Shanghai Jiao Tong University's permanent collection.

Duration: Residency November 1 to December 15, 2026 (full commitment required)

Deadline: 14 Jul 2026

International visiting artist/scholar residency at the USC-SJTU Institute of Cultural and Creative Industry in Shanghai, recruiting 5-6 participants for a cross-media, cross-cultural and cross-spatial exploration of contemporary art at a time when AI and spatial computing are reshaping artistic expression. Three program directions: Art and Technology (digital and material interaction); Humanistic Dialogue (cross-cultural understanding through exchange); and Urban Engagement (responding to urban transformation). Residents receive an individual studio plus access to ceramics, computer lab, sculpture, sound, VR and print facilities, a library and gallery, and private apartment housing (no meals). Support includes 1-2 assistants, organized museum and gallery visits, exchange with local Chinese artists, a group exhibition, and media promotion. ELIGIBILITY: teaching or public-lecture/seminar experience at a university or art institution; an active artistic practice (priority to interdisciplinary media, digital art, easel painting or integrated media); ability to organize 1-2 public events (lectures or workshops) during the residency; fluent English presentation and strong cross-cultural communication skills. Selected artists must donate two artworks for SJTU's permanent collection (provide a list of at least five works), actively participate in ICCI activities, and fully commit to the residency period. Selection announced July 30, 2026. Working languages: English and Mandarin.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eloquence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney (Culture+)

Location: Australia (participating universities)

Award: National public speaking prize (award for the winning art talk on French and Australian cultural ties). No application fee.

Duration: Three-stage competition during 2026 (university shortlist, recorded talk, national live final)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual national public speaking challenge for students at participating Australian universities, rewarding the best art talk on French and Australian cultural ties. The 2026 theme is 'Art and Environment, France and Australia: Our forests and trees in danger': students compare two artists, one Australian and one French (at least one a visual artist), analysing how a sensibility to nature shaped their work on forests and trees from the 19th century to the present. THREE STAGES: (1) submit a 400-word summary paper outlining a proposed 7-minute talk; university panels shortlist three applicants; (2) shortlisted applicants submit a recording of the full 7-minute talk and panels select one representative per university; (3) representatives compete in a live (or recorded) national final. Stage 1 summaries are judged on compliance with instructions (10%), quality of sources (15%), relevance to theme (15%), writing and structure (20%), and interest/depth/originality (40%). ELIGIBILITY: students in Arts or Humanities disciplines; in the final year of a Bachelor degree, or Honours, Graduate Certificate, Masters or PhD students; enrolled for the first semester of the year at a participating institution; over 18. Stage 1 due 11:59PM, 31 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nicholas and Angela Curtis Cite Internationale des Arts Fellowships 2026 (Curtis Artist Fellowships)
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney

Location: Paris, France (residency); applicants must be Australian

Award: $13,000 AUD towards travel and living costs, plus rent-free access to the Power Institute's living/working studio at the Cite Internationale des Arts for 3 months. Note: fellows pay a refundable bond deposit and utility costs. No application fee.

Duration: 3-month residency in one quarterly period (Jan-Mar, Apr-Jun, Jul-Sep, or Oct-Dec)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Fellowships for Australian artists to live and work at the Cite Internationale des Arts in Paris for three months, undertaking a specific artistic project using the city's institutions, exhibitions, archives, libraries, collections and artistic resources. Four fellowships are offered per year. Fellows receive rent-free access to the Power Institute's dedicated living/working studio at the Cite, $13,000 AUD towards travel and living costs (paid roughly 6 weeks before travel), and access to the Cite's facilities and global community of artists plus the broader Paris art infrastructure. Fellows pay a refundable bond and utility costs, and on return must share outcomes via a report, public event and/or exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists who are Australian citizens or Permanent Residents; applicants cannot have previously held a Cite Internationale des Arts residency (via the Power Institute or any other organisation). Awarded by a committee chaired by the Director of the Power Institute in consultation with the Cite, judged on the strength of the project, the benefit of pursuing it via a Paris residency, and the potential to further the applicant's professional development. Applications open 20 May 2026 and close midnight, 30 June 2026; applicants notified September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Virginia Spate Research Fellowships 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney

Location: Paris, France (research trip); applicants must be Australian

Award: $20,000 AUD grant for travel and living expenses (including accommodation), plus support finding accommodation. No application fee.

Duration: 3-month research trip in Paris

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Fellowships for Australian researchers to live and work in Paris for three months on a specific research project in art and visual culture, using the city's archives, collections and research networks. Two fellowships are awarded per year: Fellowship 1 for University of Sydney staff members or PhD candidates, and Fellowship 2 for all other researchers (PhD candidate level or higher, including university-based researchers at any career stage, independent researchers, museum or gallery curators, and archivists or collection specialists). Each fellowship includes a $20,000 AUD grant for travel and living expenses (paid roughly 6 weeks before travel), support in finding accommodation, and access to Paris's archives, collections and research networks. On return, fellows must share outcomes via a report and/or public event. ELIGIBILITY: open to all researchers in art and visual culture who are Australian citizens or Permanent Residents; applicants cannot have previously received a Power Institute fellowship (such as at the Cite Internationale des Arts). Awarded by a committee chaired by the Director of the Power Institute, judged on the quality and originality of the project, the relevance and accessibility of the Paris-based research resources (and why the work cannot be done remotely or from the home institution), and the potential to further the applicant's professional development. Applications open 20 May 2026 and close midnight, 30 June 2026; applicants notified September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CRP Research Fellowship Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: CRP Research Fellowship Programme

Location: Participating member countries (host institution must be in a different participating country from the applicant's home institution)

Award: Travel lump-sum allowance plus return economy airfare, a weekly subsistence allowance of EUR 600 or EUR 650 (depending on host-country cost of living), and a fixed EUR 165 terminal allowance. Not covered: insurance, visa fees, lab/bench fees, family travel, and personal or dependents' costs.

Duration: 6 to 26 weeks (short-term research placement abroad)

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

Short-term international research fellowship for scientists seeking placements at host institutions in participating member countries. Fellowships last 6 to 26 weeks and support international scientific collaboration, research mobility, knowledge exchange and cross-border partnerships. PRIORITY THEMES: sustainable agricultural productivity, climate action, environmental protection, biodiversity conservation, soil health, water resource sustainability, knowledge innovation, sustainable livestock systems, fisheries development and aquaculture sustainability. FUNDING: a travel lump sum, return economy airfare, a weekly subsistence allowance (EUR 600 or EUR 650 by host-country cost of living) and a EUR 165 terminal allowance; insurance, visa fees, lab/bench fees and family or personal costs are not covered. ELIGIBILITY: employed by or affiliated with an institution in a participating country; proposed host institution in a different participating country; at least 4 years of postdoctoral experience (exceptional candidates with equivalent expertise and a strong publication record may also be considered). Applicants must not already hold a position at the host institution, need employer approval and assurance of continued employment or affiliation after the fellowship, and previous fellows may reapply only after a 5-year gap. Applicants confirm country eligibility, secure a host institution and collaboration, prepare a research proposal (objective, scientific relevance, methodology, expected outcomes, 6-26 week timeline, collaboration benefits) and submit supporting documents (CV, publication list, employer approval, host acceptance letter, proof of affiliation).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Day Job: Short Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Day Job Films

Location: Worldwide applicants; all production must take place in the UK

Award: Production budget of up to GBP 7,000, with full creative control and no producer or external interference. No application fee.

Duration: Per short film project

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

Short film competition from Day Job Films created to give auteur directors full creative freedom, awarding a production budget of up to GBP 7,000 to make a short film with no producer interference or commercial agenda. Open to animation, live-action and documentary projects. Runs in three rounds: (1) a pitch deck outlining concept, story, key characters and visual style; (2) a full script for those who progress; and (3) an interview to discuss the vision and execution. A panel of industry professionals judges the pitch deck, script and interview, with at least one team member reading each pitch deck and script in full. The winner receives full creative control over the project. ELIGIBILITY: applicants can be from anywhere worldwide, but all production must take place in the UK; applicants must have a producer on board who is separate from the director/writer. Applicants may optionally include a plan for adapting the script into a feature-length film (this does not affect the outcome). Submissions open February 10, 2026 and close August 10, 2026. Enquiries: hello@dayjobfilms.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Blackstone Publishing Novel Initiative 2026 (with The Black List)
item.description:
Organisation: The Black List and Blackstone Publishing

Location: Online (manuscripts hosted on blcklst.com)

Award: A $25,000 publishing deal with Blackstone Publishing for the selected manuscript.

Duration: Submission period November 20, 2025 to June 9, 2026

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

A partnership between The Black List and Blackstone Publishing to identify an unpublished manuscript for a $25,000 publishing deal. The Black List helps Blackstone shortlist outstanding manuscripts submitted on blcklst.com during the submission period. Open to manuscripts in all genres, with particular interest in work that elevates familiar themes (love, adventure, guilt, desire, betrayal, family, identity, obsession) from fresh, compelling perspectives, especially thrillers, high-concept mysteries, romances and horror. REQUIREMENTS: post an original manuscript on blcklst.com, receive at least one evaluation while hosted there (once evaluated, a manuscript can be submitted to any eligible partnership at no additional cost), and opt in to the program during the submission period. Submitted manuscripts must not be under contract elsewhere; no feature, pilot, play or musical submissions are eligible. Applicants must be at least 18 and the sole owner of all rights to wholly original material, and must agree to the Submission Agreement (which includes legally binding terms such as arbitration). If selected, the writer may enter into a book deal negotiated in good faith. Writing teams are allowed, but every member must comply and all must opt out if any member becomes ineligible. Note: submitting requires being logged in to blcklst.com with an uploaded project (the site charges separately for hosting and evaluations). Deadline June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: KAMP: Creative Body Institute Residency, Lesbos, Greece 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Body Institute (CBI)

Location: Lesbos Island, Greece (eco-art hermitage in an olive grove on Mount Lepetymnos)

Award: Co-funded residency. Total cost per artist EUR 2,500; CBI funds EUR 1,350, so the artist contribution is EUR 1,150 (plus a EUR 100 non-refundable deposit deducted from that contribution). Does not include travel, visas, airport transfers, insurance or personal food. One full contribution waiver is available per cohort (request on the form; reviewed after selection, based on financial need and cohort balance).

Duration: Roughly 3-week residency per track; tracks run July 22 to September 26, 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

KAMP is a communal residency from the artist-run non-profit Creative Body Institute, inviting queer-identifying artists to an ecological hermitage on Lesbos Island, Greece, to develop work exploring queer ecologies, queer temporality, and ecofeminist practices through a human and more-than-human, life-art ethos. CBI seeks artists whose practices engage embodiment, interdependence and temporality through performance, installation, sound, dance or experimental visual work. FOUR TRACKS: (1) Experimental Media and Performativity, Jul 22-Aug 10; (2) Relational and Site-Responsive Work, Aug 14-Sep 2; (3) Body-Based and Durational Practice, Sep 6-24/26; (4) Writer in Residence (dates coinciding with one of the above, for those focused on exegesis, a publication, or writing about prior embodied/artistic research). 5-7 artists are selected per cohort. Housing is simple cabin-based accommodation for relational living (Queer Family Cabin for 2-3, Cozy Cabin for solitude, Sanctuary Cabin for 2 sharing), with communal outdoor kitchen, shower house and bathrooms; this is an off-grid, sustainable environment (solar power, conscious water use, limited wifi). Artists must be comfortable with close communal living, shared rhythms, a collective food system (primarily vegetarian and pescatarian, sourced from local farmers), and unpaved terrain (which may be difficult for those with mobility impairments). INCLUDED: accommodation, shared indoor/outdoor workspaces, groceries for shared meals and staff support, two on-site CBI artist-faculty, mentorship and feedback, structured and informal group programming, work-in-progress sharing, and photo/video documentation. APPLICATION: online form (no draft saving) with project description (350 words) tied to the chosen track, 1-2 work samples, optional portfolio, artist bio(s) (350 words), and three short-answer questions; you may respond in any language. Apply individually (willing to share), as a duo, or as a trio/small group. Deadline May 24, 2026 (23:59 CET); virtual interviews May 25-29; final notifications June 1. Confirm within 5 days of acceptance with the EUR 100 deposit.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BRIClab: Film + TV Residency 2026/27 (Brooklyn)
item.description:
Organisation: BRIC

Location: Brooklyn, New York, USA (NYC-based)

Award: $2,500 stipend plus up to $2,000 in production costs, alongside equipment and post-production access and mentorship. No application fee.

Duration: Residency across 2026/27

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

BRIClab Film + TV residency at BRIC in Brooklyn, supporting emerging-to-mid-career documentary filmmakers to develop a project with a stipend, production funds, equipment and post-production access, and mentorship. ELIGIBILITY: NYC-based documentary filmmakers, individuals or duos; the programme prioritises disabled, BIPOC and LGBTQ+ artists. Apply via the BRIC opportunity page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: John Hansard Gallery: Open Call for Disabled Artists 2026 (Future Curators)
item.description:
Organisation: John Hansard Gallery, with the University of Southampton (part of the DASH Future Curators Programme)

Location: Within 40 miles of John Hansard Gallery, Southampton, UK

Award: GBP 2,000 artist fee (including up to four meetings with students), plus travel expenses, access costs and a materials budget to be defined. No application fee.

Duration: Project running July 2026 to a February 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Open call from John Hansard Gallery for one disabled artist to collaborate with Occupational Therapy and Engineering students at the University of Southampton to make or adapt tools that support their artistic practice. The tools and resulting artwork are displayed in an exhibition at John Hansard Gallery in February 2027, curated by Jess Starns as part of the Future Curators Programme, a disability-justice residency programme for Disabled curators founded by the Disabled artist-led organisation DASH. The work involves collaborating with students in July and August, testing and refining the tools through autumn, and creating artwork for the exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: disabled artists living and working within a 40-mile distance of John Hansard Gallery. Submit an Expression of Interest via the gallery's open-call page; access requirements supported (contact jennifa@jhg.art).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pulitzer Center: Global Reporting Grants
item.description:
Organisation: Pulitzer Center

Location: Remote / field-based (international reporting; open worldwide)

Award: Most awards $5,000 to $10,000 per project, covering reporting hard costs (typically no salaries or equipment). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Pulitzer Center reporting grants supporting in-depth international journalism on under-reported global issues. Open to reporters, photographers, audio and video journalists, and documentary filmmakers worldwide. Grants cover the hard costs of reporting projects. ELIGIBILITY: professional journalists and visual storytellers anywhere in the world; both freelance and staff. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis via the Pulitzer Center grant portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pulitzer Center: U.S. Local News Reporting Grants (Bringing Stories Home)
item.description:
Organisation: Pulitzer Center

Location: United States (US-based stories)

Award: Reporting grants covering project hard costs (typically no salaries or equipment). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Pulitzer Center's 'Bringing Stories Home' initiative supports in-depth local journalism on under-covered issues in communities across the United States. ELIGIBILITY: freelance and staff journalists working on US-based stories. Grants cover the hard costs of reporting. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis via the Pulitzer Center reporting-grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Film London: The FLAMIN Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Film London (FLAMIN - Film London Artists' Moving Image Network), with Arts Council England

Location: England, UK (year-long programme with in-person workshops)

Award: GBP 2,500 project-development bursary per artist, plus a year-long programme of monthly in-person workshops, 1:1 sessions with a production advisor, screening and promotion, and a peer community. Stage-2 interviewees also receive a GBP 100 interview fee. No application fee.

Duration: One-year fellowship programme

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

The FLAMIN Fellowship from Film London supports early-career artists working in moving image with a development bursary and a structured year-long career-development programme. ELIGIBILITY: England-based moving-image artists with roughly 2 to 5 years of professional practice outside higher education and at least two prior exhibited or screened works; excludes current students, gallery-represented artists, those with major solo institutional shows, and film-industry professionals. Apply via the Film London FLAMIN Fellowship page by midnight on 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Zoyd Studios: Electroacoustic & Electronic Music Residency 2026/2027
item.description:
Organisation: Art Zoyd Studios

Location: Valenciennes, France (in-person residency)

Award: Funded residency: accommodation and meals covered during the residency, travel reimbursed up to EUR 400, and support from a computer-music designer; a copyright bonus is possible. No artist fee or stipend. No application fee.

Duration: Residency across the 2026/2027 season

Deadline: 29 May 2026

Production residency at Art Zoyd Studios in Valenciennes for composers, musicians and sound artists working in electroacoustic and electronic music, with technical support from a computer-music designer to develop new work. ELIGIBILITY: composers, musicians and artists practising in electroacoustic or electronic music; open worldwide. Apply via the Art Zoyd Studios residency request page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Letras Boricuas Fellowship 2026 (5th and final cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Flamboyan Arts Fund, in partnership with the Mellon Foundation

Location: Puerto Rico and its diaspora (remote; writers with sustained ties to Puerto Rico)

Award: $25,000 unrestricted award to each of 24 fellows. No application fee.

Duration: One-off unrestricted fellowship

Deadline: Closed 22 May 2026

Fifth and final cohort of the Letras Boricuas Fellowship, awarding unrestricted support to writers with deep, sustained ties to Puerto Rico and its diaspora. ELIGIBILITY: emerging and established writers across fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry, spoken word and playwriting, who demonstrate a sustained connection to Puerto Rico and its diaspora. Apply via the Flamboyan Arts Fund portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Grantham Foundation: Creation Residency in Visual Arts 2026 (Quebec)
item.description:
Organisation: Fondation Grantham pour l'art et l'environnement

Location: Saint-Edmond-de-Grantham, Quebec, Canada (4-week on-site residency)

Award: CAD $5,000 to $10,000 grant plus a four-week residency (accommodation and studio provided). No application fee.

Duration: Four-week residency

Deadline: 27 May 2026

Creation residency from the Grantham Foundation for art and the environment, pairing a four-week residency with a production grant for work engaging environmental subjects or approaches. ELIGIBILITY: established visual artists with at least five years of professional experience; open to artists based in Quebec or abroad; duos are eligible. Apply via the foundation's residency page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence 2026 (Oak Spring)
item.description:
Organisation: Oak Spring Garden Foundation

Location: Upperville, Virginia, USA (2-5 week on-site residency)

Award: $10,000 grant plus a two- to five-week residency with accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two- to five-week residency

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence from the Oak Spring Garden Foundation, supporting early-career artists whose work engages plants, gardens and landscapes. ELIGIBILITY: early-career visual and literary artists, dancers and musicians; no nationality restriction stated, though the residency takes place on site in Virginia. Apply via the Oak Spring fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN Presents x SALT: Sample Translation Grants 2026 (South Asian languages)
item.description:
Organisation: English PEN, in partnership with SALT

Location: Remote (worldwide translators; source works in South Asian languages)

Award: GBP 500 to each of 24 shortlisted translators for a 5,000-word sample translation; 12 winners additionally receive editorial support and promotion. No application fee.

Duration: One-off sample-translation commission

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Special PEN Presents round in partnership with SALT, funding sample translations from South Asian languages to help unpublished works find English-language publishers. The scheme pays for the sample-translation work that is usually unpaid. ELIGIBILITY: individual literary translators at any career stage, working from source languages of Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, the Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan or Sri Lanka. Apply via the English PEN PEN Presents page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pulitzer Center: Rainforest Investigations Network Fellowship 2026 (6th cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Pulitzer Center

Location: Field-based reporting across the Amazon, Congo Basin and Southeast Asia

Award: Yearlong fellowship covering the fellow's salary, plus editorial collaboration and reporting support through the Rainforest Investigations Network. No application fee.

Duration: One-year fellowship

Deadline: Closed 22 May 2026

Yearlong investigative journalism fellowship from the Pulitzer Center's Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN), recruiting its sixth cohort. Fellows pursue in-depth investigations into the drivers of deforestation and degradation in the world's three major tropical rainforest regions: the Amazon, the Congo Basin and Southeast Asia. ELIGIBILITY: experienced investigative journalists based in or covering these regions; the network operates across multiple languages, so multilingual applicants are welcome. The fellowship covers the fellow's salary for the year alongside editorial support.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Open Call Fresh Perspectives 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands (Dutch fund; cross-sector collaboration)

Award: Two-phase grant. Phase 1: ten grants of EUR 10,000 (max 5 months, startup 1 December 2026 to 30 April 2027) to prepare, explore and prototype the project. Phase 2: ten grants of EUR 50,000 (max 12 months, implementation 1 September 2027 to 31 August 2028) for projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1. No application fee.

Duration: Phase 1 max 5 months; Phase 2 (if selected) max 12 months

Deadline: 25 Aug 2026

Open call from the Creative Industries Fund NL (Stimuleringsfonds) for cross-sector collaboration projects that offer new perspectives on current challenges such as climate, housing, polarisation, migration or inequality of opportunity. The procedure runs in two phases. In Phase 1, the applicant and partner use the period to prepare and kick off the project, further explore the theme, intended end product and collaboration, and investigate possibilities (including working on a prototype or proof of concept), concluding with a project plan, budget and collaboration agreement. Phase 2, open only to projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1, focuses on implementation and requires both a written application and a presentation to the advisory committee; projects may deliver various end products (an activity, intervention, event, publication, service, product or process) accompanied by a suitable form of knowledge sharing such as a presentation, symposium or publication. ELIGIBILITY: applicants working with at least one partner in cross-sector collaboration; see the fund's open-call page for full eligibility details. Deadline 25 August 2026, 16:00 CEST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SSRC: Innovation in Religion and Spirituality Seed Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Social Science Research Council (SSRC) - Religion and the Public Sphere program, with support from the Luce Foundation, the John Templeton Foundation, and the Templeton World Charity Foundation

Location: Remote (research grant; no specific location requirement)

Award: Up to USD 10,000 per award. Funds may cover travel and accommodation, research equipment and supplies, research assistants, and access to publications or proprietary databases (other uses possible in exceptional cases, in consultation with programme staff). No application fee.

Duration: Up to six months (project period September 2026 to February 2027)

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Seed grant competition from the SSRC's Religion and the Public Sphere program for research examining the dynamics of religious and spiritual change through the frame of 'innovation', understood as the strategic recombination of existing elements within broader social, political and cultural change rather than invention ex nihilo. Possible topics include innovations within transnational or diasporic communities (especially circulation to and from the Global South); gendered knowledge of innovation; how innovations attain (or fail to attain) legitimacy and authority; the relationship between contemporary economic conditions and religious/spiritual innovation; historical studies of innovations and their longevity; and civic participation or social mobilisation of new religious identities. ELIGIBILITY: scholars working as professional researchers, postdoctoral researchers, university faculty, or doctoral students who have advanced to candidacy. Open to all social science fields (anthropology, economics, geography, history, political science, sociology) as well as humanities, theology and other relevant fields; qualitative, quantitative or mixed methods. All materials must be submitted in English. Applications consist of a research proposal, application form, detailed budget and brief CV, submitted via the SSRC online portal by 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time, 1 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Council 90th Anniversary Research Fellowships at IASH 2027 (Edinburgh)
item.description:
Organisation: Institute for Advanced Studies in the Humanities (IASH), University of Edinburgh, in partnership with the British Council

Location: Edinburgh, UK (10 months residential at IASH; final 2 months in home country)

Award: GBP 2,500 per month bursary for 12 months, plus travel expenses, dedicated office space, University of Edinburgh email and library access, a University mentor, weekly Fellows' Lunch, work-in-progress seminars, full calendar of Institute and College events, and opportunities to participate in or design funded workshops and colloquia. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months (10 months residential at IASH, January to October 2027; followed by November-December 2027 knowledge-exchange in the home country)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Twelve-month postdoctoral fellowship from the Institute for Advanced Studies in the Humanities (IASH) at the University of Edinburgh, in partnership with the British Council, marking the Council's 90th anniversary. Up to two fellowships per year as part of the 2025-2027 partnership. Fellows spend ten months residential at IASH (January to October 2027) followed by up to two months of knowledge-exchange and dissemination work in their home country in collaboration with the British Council. ELIGIBILITY: postdoctoral researchers based in an ODA-recipient country where the British Council operates (full list of ~60+ countries published on the IASH page, spanning Albania to Zimbabwe and including the Occupied Palestinian Territories); PhD completed within the last seven years (career breaks excluded from the seven-year window); applicants must not hold a permanent university position and must not have held a prior IASH Fellowship. Research themes should align with British Council priorities across Arts, Education and English language, plus cross-cutting interests in international relations, soft power, international development, peace building, and cultural relations and diplomacy. NOTE: applicants are required to contact relevant University of Edinburgh researchers before submitting; informational webinar 26 May 2026. Decisions communicated late September 2026. References (minimum two, maximum three) must be emailed by referees directly to iash@ed.ac.uk by the deadline.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tractor Beam: Issue 6 'The Water Issue' - Call for Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Tractor Beam

Location: Online publication (remote submissions; geographic eligibility not stated)

Award: $1,000 USD per accepted submission. Accepted work is considered for publication online (Tractor Beam website and Substack) and, in some cases, for one-off printed editions. Stories may also be considered for the Protopian Prize with contributor consent. No submission fee mentioned.

Duration: One-off published piece (no residency component)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Tractor Beam's sixth issue, themed around water in soil, growth, land and ecosystems large and small, is open for submissions. Editors are seeking anti-apocalyptic visions that explore the future of water in farming and food production, island ecologies, hybrid sea-soil technologies, the people who move water and the people water moves, plus stories about drought, diaspora and what gets carried downstream. FORMAT: stories under 6,000 words; comics 12-16 panels. Submissions accepted via the call page on Tractor Beam's Substack.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
